summary: cdk-insights 1.42.3 contains worm-style auto-publish and exfiltration patterns
details: The package ships code that combines hardcoded outbound POST endpoints, host reconnaissance (ping), and an automated `npm publish` loop driven by `writeFileSync`. dist/entry.js (around lines 11–25) issues hardcoded POST requests; dist/aspects/CdkInsightsAspect.js (line 70) invokes `ping` alongside outbound POST traffic; dist/entry.js (line 299) and dist/index.js (line 80) write package files and then invoke `npm publish` repeatedly. The combined shape — host probing, hardcoded outbound C2-style HTTP, and an automated package-republish primitive — matches the structural fingerprint of a self-propagating supply-chain worm that exfiltrates installer data and republishes malicious versions under the victim's npm identity. Installing or loading this package risks credential/host-data exfiltration plus enumeration and republication of the developer's other npm packages.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
